title: Alfred Wegener: science, exploration and the theory of continental drift
subtitle: by Mott T Greene

phrases.def: 古气候学（研究古代气候）

sentences.en: This is a book about the life and scientific work of Alfred Wegener, whose reputation today rests with his theory of continental displacements, better known as ‘continental drift’.
sentences.zh: 这是一本关于阿尔弗雷德·魏格纳的生平及其科学工作的书，他如今的声誉建立在其“大陆位移”理论之上，该理论更为人熟知的名称是“大陆漂移”。

grammar.type: 非限制性定语从句 + 过去分词短语
grammar.note: 主干 This is a book about...；whose reputation today rests with his theory of continental displacements 为 whose 引导的非限制性定语从句；better known as 'continental drift' 为过去分词短语作后置定语，补充说明该理论的通称。

sentences.en: Wegener proposed this theory in 1912 and developed it extensively for nearly 20 years.
sentences.zh: 魏格纳于1912年提出了这一理论，并在近20年间对其进行了大量的发展和完善。

grammar.type: 一般过去时 + 并列谓语
grammar.note: 主语 Wegener 带两个并列谓语 proposed... 与 developed...；extensively 为副词修饰 developed；for nearly 20 years 为时间状语。

sentences.en: His book on the subject, The Origin of Continents and Oceans, went through four editions and was the focus of an international controversy in his lifetime and for some years after his death.
sentences.zh: 他关于这一主题的著作《海陆的起源》历经四版，在他生前以及去世后的若干年里都是一场国际争论的焦点。

grammar.type: 同位语 + 并列谓语
grammar.note: The Origin of Continents and Oceans 为 His book on the subject 的同位语；两个并列谓语 went through four editions 与 was the focus of an international controversy；in his lifetime and for some years after his death 为并列时间状语。

sentences.en: Wegener’s basic idea was that many mysteries about the Earth’s history could be solved if one supposed that the continents moved laterally, rather than supposing that they remained fixed in place.
sentences.zh: 魏格纳的基本观点是：如果人们假定大陆是横向移动的，而非假定它们固定不动，那么许多关于地球历史的谜团便可迎刃而解。

grammar.type: 表语从句 + 内嵌条件从句 + rather than 对比
grammar.note: 主干 Wegener's basic idea was that...，that 引导表语从句；从句内含 if one supposed that... 条件从句；rather than supposing that they remained fixed in place 用 rather than + 动名词构成对比。

sentences.en: Wegener showed in great detail how such continental movements were plausible and how they worked, using evidence from a large number of sciences including geology, geophysics, paleontology, and climatology.
sentences.zh: 魏格纳极为详尽地论证了这样的大陆运动如何是合理可信的、又是如何发生的，其依据来自地质学、地球物理学、古生物学和气候学等众多学科的证据。

grammar.type: 宾语从句并列 + 现在分词状语
grammar.note: 主干 Wegener showed in great detail how... and how...，两个 how 引导并列宾语从句；using evidence from... 为现在分词短语作方式状语；including... 列举所涉学科。

sentences.en: Wegener’s idea – that the continents move – is at the heart of the theory that guides Earth sciences today: namely plate tectonics.
sentences.zh: 魏格纳的观点——大陆是移动的——正处于当今指导地球科学的那一理论的核心，即板块构造学说。

grammar.type: 破折号同位语 + 定语从句 + namely 补充
grammar.note: 两个破折号之间 that the continents move 为 Wegener's idea 的同位语从句；主句 is at the heart of the theory；that guides Earth sciences today 为定语从句修饰 theory；冒号后 namely plate tectonics 点明该理论名称。

sentences.en: Plate tectonics is in many respects quite different from Wegener’s proposal, in the same way that modern evolutionary theory is very different from the ideas Charles Darwin proposed in the 1850s about biological evolution.
sentences.zh: 板块构造学说在许多方面与魏格纳的设想颇为不同，正如现代进化论与查尔斯·达尔文19世纪50年代提出的生物进化观点大相径庭一样。

grammar.type: in the same way that 比较状语从句
grammar.note: 主干 Plate tectonics is... quite different from Wegener's proposal；in the same way that... 引导方式比较状语从句；从句内 the ideas (that) Charles Darwin proposed... 含省略关系词的定语从句。

sentences.en: Yet plate tectonics is a descendant of Alfred Wegener’s theory of continental drift, in quite the same way that modern evolutionary theory is a descendant of Darwin’s theory of natural selection.
sentences.zh: 然而，板块构造学说是阿尔弗雷德·魏格纳大陆漂移理论的后继者，正如现代进化论是达尔文自然选择理论的后继者一样。

grammar.type: 转折 Yet + 比较状语从句
grammar.note: Yet 表转折；主干 plate tectonics is a descendant of... theory of continental drift；in quite the same way that... 引导方式比较从句，将两组“后继关系”并置类比。

sentences.en: When I started writing about Wegener’s life and work, one of the most intriguing things about him for me was that, although he came up with a theory on continental drift, he was not a geologist.
sentences.zh: 当我开始撰写魏格纳的生平与工作时，对我而言，他身上最耐人寻味的一点是：尽管他提出了一套大陆漂移理论，他却并不是一位地质学家。

grammar.type: 时间状语从句 + 表语从句 + 让步从句
grammar.note: When I started writing... 为时间状语从句；主干 one of the most intriguing things... was that...，that 引导表语从句；从句内 although he came up with a theory... 为让步状语从句。

words.def: 引人入胜的；耐人寻味的

sentences.en: He trained as an astronomer and pursued a career in atmospheric physics.
sentences.zh: 他所受的训练是成为一名天文学家，其职业生涯则投身于大气物理学。

grammar.type: 并列谓语
grammar.note: 主语 He 带两个并列谓语 trained as an astronomer 与 pursued a career in atmospheric physics；train as 意为“接受……的训练/培养为”。

words.def: 从事某职业；追求事业发展

sentences.en: When he proposed the theory of continental displacements in 1912, he was a lecturer in physics and astronomy at the University of Marburg, in southern Germany.
sentences.zh: 1912年他提出大陆位移理论时，正在德国南部的马尔堡大学担任物理学和天文学讲师。

grammar.type: 时间状语从句 + 主系表
grammar.note: When he proposed... in 1912 为时间状语从句；主句 he was a lecturer in physics and astronomy at the University of Marburg；in southern Germany 为地点同位/补充状语。

sentences.en: However, he was not an ‘unknown’.
sentences.zh: 然而，他并非一个“无名之辈”。

grammar.type: 主系表 + 转折状语
grammar.note: However 为转折副词；主干 he was not an 'unknown'，此处 unknown 由形容词转作名词，指“无名之人”，加引号表特殊含义。

sentences.en: In 1906 he had set a world record (with his brother Kurt) for time aloft in a hot-air balloon: 52 hours.
sentences.zh: 1906年，他（与弟弟库尔特一起）创下了热气球滞空时间的世界纪录：52小时。

grammar.type: 过去完成时 + 冒号补充
grammar.note: had set a world record 用过去完成时，表在前述1912年之前已发生；括号内 with his brother Kurt 为补充；冒号后 52 hours 具体说明 the record 的数值。

sentences.en: Between 1906 and 1908 he had taken part in a highly publicized and extremely dangerous expedition to the coast of northeast Greenland.
sentences.zh: 1906年至1908年间，他曾参加一次广受宣传且极其危险的探险，前往格陵兰东北海岸。

grammar.type: 过去完成时 + 多重定语
grammar.note: had taken part in 用过去完成时；a highly publicized and extremely dangerous expedition 中两个形容词并列作定语；to the coast of northeast Greenland 为介词短语作后置定语说明探险目的地。

sentences.en: He had also made a name for himself amongst a small circle of meteorologists and atmospheric physicists in Germany as the author of a textbook, Thermodynamics of the Atmosphere (1911), and of a number of interesting scientific papers.
sentences.zh: 作为教科书《大气热力学》（1911年）以及若干有趣科学论文的作者，他还在德国一小圈子的气象学家和大气物理学家中为自己赢得了名声。

grammar.type: 过去完成时 + as 短语 + of 并列
grammar.note: 主干 He had also made a name for himself amongst...；as the author of a textbook... and of a number of... papers 为 as 引导的身份状语，两个 of 短语并列表明其著作；Thermodynamics of the Atmosphere (1911) 为 textbook 同位语。

sentences.en: As important as Wegener’s work on continental drift has turned out to be, it was largely a sideline to his interest in atmospheric physics, geophysics, and paleoclimatology, and thus I have been at great pains to put Wegener’s work on continental drift in the larger context of his other scientific work, and in the even larger context of atmospheric sciences in his lifetime.
sentences.zh: 尽管事实证明魏格纳在大陆漂移方面的工作极为重要，但它在很大程度上只是他对大气物理学、地球物理学和古气候学之兴趣的一条支线；因此，我煞费苦心地将魏格纳关于大陆漂移的工作置于其其他科学工作的更大背景之中，并置于他所处时代大气科学这一更为宏大的背景之下。

grammar.type: As...as 让步倒装 + 结果并列
grammar.note: As important as... has turned out to be 为 as...as 引导的让步状语（形容词提前形成让步倒装，相当于 Although）；主句 it was largely a sideline to...；and thus 引出结果分句 I have been at great pains to put... in the larger context of..., and in the even larger context of...。

sentences.en: This is a ‘continental drift book’ only to the extent that Wegener was interested in that topic and later became famous for it.
sentences.zh: 这本书之所以算作一本“大陆漂移之书”，仅限于魏格纳对该主题感兴趣、并在后来因之成名这一范围之内。

grammar.type: to the extent that 程度状语从句
grammar.note: 主干 This is a 'continental drift book'；only to the extent that... 引导程度状语从句，限定“在何种程度上成立”；从句内 was interested in... and later became famous for it 两谓语并列。

sentences.en: My treatment of his other scientific work is no less detailed, though I certainly have devoted more attention to the reception of his ideas on continental displacement, as they were much more controversial than his other work.
sentences.zh: 我对他其他科学工作的论述同样详尽，尽管我确实对其大陆位移思想所受到的反响倾注了更多关注，因为这些思想比他的其他工作更具争议性。

grammar.type: no less...than 隐含 + though 让步 + as 原因
grammar.note: 主句 My treatment... is no less detailed（同样详尽）；though I certainly have devoted more attention to... 为让步从句；as they were much more controversial than his other work 为 as 引导的原因状语从句。

sentences.en: Readers interested in the specific detail of Wegener’s career will see that he often stopped pursuing a given line of investigation (sometimes for years on end), only to pick it up later.
sentences.zh: 对魏格纳职业生涯具体细节感兴趣的读者会发现，他常常中止某一研究方向（有时一停就是数年），却又在日后重新拾起。

grammar.type: 后置定语 + 宾语从句 + only to 结果
grammar.note: Readers interested in... 为形容词短语作后置定语修饰 Readers；主干 Readers... will see that...，that 引导宾语从句；only to pick it up later 为不定式表出乎意料的结果，for years on end 意为“连续数年”。

sentences.en: I have tried to provide guideposts to his rapidly shifting interests by characterizing different phases of his life as careers in different sciences, which is reflected in the titles of the chapters.
sentences.zh: 我试图为他迅速变换的兴趣提供路标，方法是把他人生的不同阶段刻画为不同科学领域中的“职业生涯”，这一点也反映在各章的标题之中。

grammar.type: by 动名词方式状语 + 非限制性定语从句
grammar.note: 主干 I have tried to provide guideposts to...；by characterizing different phases... as careers... 为方式状语（characterize A as B）；which is reflected in the titles of the chapters 为非限制性定语从句，指代前述做法。

sentences.en: Thus, the index should be a sufficient guide for those interested in a particular aspect of Wegener’s life but perhaps not all of it.
sentences.zh: 因此，对于那些只对魏格纳人生某一特定方面、而非全部感兴趣的读者来说，索引应当是一个足够好的指引。

grammar.type: 主系表 + 后置定语 + but 对比
grammar.note: 主干 the index should be a sufficient guide for those；interested in a particular aspect... but perhaps not all of it 为形容词短语作 those 的后置定语，用 but 形成“某一方面”与“全部”的对比。

sentences.en: My own feeling, however, is that the parts do not make as much sense on their own as do all of his activities taken together.
sentences.zh: 然而，我个人的感受是：这些部分单独来看，并不像把他所有活动合在一起看那样有意义。

grammar.type: 表语从句 + as...as 比较倒装
grammar.note: 主干 My own feeling... is that...，that 引导表语从句；从句内 do not make as much sense on their own as do all of his activities taken together，第二个 as 后用 do... 的部分倒装，taken together 为过去分词短语作定语。

sentences.en: In this respect I urge readers to try to experience Wegener’s life as he lived it, with all the interruptions, changes of mind, and renewed efforts this entailed.
sentences.zh: 在这一点上，我力劝读者试着如魏格纳本人所经历的那样去体验他的一生，连同其中所包含的种种中断、改变主意和重新努力。

grammar.type: urge sb to do + as 方式从句 + 省略关系词定语从句
grammar.note: 主干 I urge readers to try to experience Wegener's life；as he lived it 为方式状语从句；with all the interruptions, changes of mind, and renewed efforts 为伴随状语，其后 (that) this entailed 为省略关系词的定语从句。

sentences.en: Wegener left behind a few published works but, as was standard practice, these reported the results of his work – not the journey he took to reach that point.
sentences.zh: 魏格纳身后留下了少量已发表的著作，但正如当时的惯常做法，这些著作只报告其研究的结果——而非他抵达那一结论所走过的历程。

grammar.type: but 并列 + as 非限制从句 + 破折号对比
grammar.note: 前一分句 Wegener left behind a few published works；but 连接后一分句 these reported the results of his work；as was standard practice 为 as 引导的非限制性定语从句（插入）；破折号后 not the journey (that) he took... 与 the results 形成对比。

sentences.en: Only a few hundred of the many thousands of letters he wrote and received in his lifetime have survived and he didn’t keep notebooks or diaries that recorded his life and activities.
sentences.zh: 在他一生中所写和收到的成千上万封信件里，仅有几百封留存下来，而且他也没有记录自己生活与活动的笔记本或日记。

grammar.type: 省略关系词定语从句 + and 并列 + 定语从句
grammar.note: 前一分句主语 Only a few hundred of the many thousands of letters (that) he wrote and received...，含省略关系词定语从句，谓语 have survived；and 连接后一分句 he didn't keep notebooks or diaries；that recorded his life and activities 为定语从句修饰 notebooks or diaries。

sentences.en: He was not active (with a few exceptions) in scientific societies, and did not seek to find influence or advance his ideas through professional contacts and politics, spending most of his time at home in his study reading and writing, or in the field collecting observations.
sentences.zh: 他在科学团体中并不活跃（少数情况除外），也不寻求通过职业人脉和政治手段去获取影响力或推广自己的观点，而是把大部分时间用在家中书房里读书写作，或在野外采集观测资料。

grammar.type: 并列谓语 + 现在分词状语
grammar.note: 主干含两个并列谓语 was not active... and did not seek to find influence or advance his ideas...；spending most of his time... 为现在分词短语作伴随状语，其中 at home in his study reading and writing 与 in the field collecting observations 由 or 并列。

sentences.en: Some famous scientists, such as Newton, Darwin, and Einstein, left mountains of written material behind, hundreds of notebooks and letters numbering in the tens of thousands.
sentences.zh: 一些著名科学家，如牛顿、达尔文和爱因斯坦，身后留下了堆积如山的书面材料——数以百计的笔记本，以及数以万计的信件。

grammar.type: 同位举例 + 名词短语补充 + 现在分词
grammar.note: 主干 Some famous scientists... left mountains of written material behind；such as Newton, Darwin, and Einstein 为举例插入；hundreds of notebooks and letters 为对 material 的补充说明，numbering in the tens of thousands 为现在分词短语作定语。

sentences.en: Others, like Michael Faraday, left extensive journals of their thoughts and speculations, parallel to their scientific notebooks.
sentences.zh: 另一些人，如迈克尔·法拉第，则留下了记录其思想与思辨的大量日志，与其科学笔记本并行。

grammar.type: 同位举例 + 形容词短语状语
grammar.note: 主干 Others... left extensive journals of their thoughts and speculations；like Michael Faraday 为举例插入；parallel to their scientific notebooks 为形容词短语作后置定语/状语，表“与……并行、平行”。

sentences.en: The more such material a scientist leaves behind, the better chance a biographer has of forming an accurate picture of how a scientist’s ideas took shape and evolved.
sentences.zh: 一位科学家留下的此类材料越多，传记作者就越有可能准确描绘出这位科学家的思想是如何形成并演变的。

grammar.type: The more...the better... 比例结构 + 宾语从句
grammar.note: The more such material a scientist leaves behind, the better chance a biographer has of...，为“the+比较级…, the+比较级…”的比例结构；of forming an accurate picture of how a scientist's ideas took shape and evolved，其中 how... 为介词宾语从句，took shape and evolved 两谓语并列。

sentences.en: I am firmly of the opinion that most of us, Wegener included, are not in any real sense the authors of our own lives.
sentences.zh: 我坚定地认为，我们大多数人——包括魏格纳在内——在任何真正意义上都不是自己人生的作者。

grammar.type: be of the opinion that 同位语从句 + 独立主格
grammar.note: 主干 I am firmly of the opinion that...，that 引导同位语从句说明 opinion；从句主语 most of us，Wegener included 为独立主格结构作插入补充；in any real sense 为程度状语。

sentences.en: We plan, think, and act, often with apparent freedom, but most of the time our lives ‘happen to us’, and we only retrospectively turn this happenstance into a coherent narrative of fulfilled intentions.
sentences.zh: 我们制定计划、思考、行动，常常带着一种表面上的自由，但大多数时候我们的人生是“发生在我们身上”的，而我们只是在事后才把这种偶然性整理成一段“意图得以实现”的连贯叙事。

grammar.type: 并列谓语 + but 转折 + and 并列
grammar.note: 第一分句 We plan, think, and act，三动词并列，with apparent freedom 为伴随状语；but 引出转折 our lives 'happen to us'；and 连接 we only retrospectively turn this happenstance into a coherent narrative...，turn A into B 结构。

sentences.en: This book, therefore, is a story both of the life and scientific work that Alfred Wegener planned and intended and of the life and scientific work that actually ‘happened to him’.
sentences.zh: 因此，本书既是一个关于阿尔弗雷德·魏格纳所计划、所意图的人生与科学工作的故事，也是一个关于实际上“发生在他身上”的人生与科学工作的故事。

grammar.type: both...and... 并列 + 定语从句
grammar.note: 主干 This book... is a story both of... and of...，both...and... 并列两个 of 短语；每个 of 短语后各含一个 that 引导的定语从句：that Alfred Wegener planned and intended 与 that actually 'happened to him'。

sentences.en: These are, as I think you will soon see, not always the same thing.
sentences.zh: 正如我想你很快就会明白的，这两者并不总是同一回事。

grammar.type: 主系表 + as 非限制性从句插入
grammar.note: 主干 These are... not always the same thing；as I think you will soon see 为 as 引导的非限制性定语从句作插入语，as 指代整句内容。

questions.title: Questions 27–30
instructions: Do the following statements agree with the claims of the writer in Reading Passage 3?
In boxes 27–30 on your answer sheet, write
YES if the statement agrees with the claims of the writer
NO if the statement contradicts the claims of the writer
NOT GIVEN if it is impossible to say what the writer thinks about this

items.prompt: Wegener’s ideas about continental drift were widely disputed while he was alive.
The idea that the continents remained fixed in place was defended in a number of respected scientific publications.
Wegener relied on a limited range of scientific fields to support his theory of continental drift.
The similarities between Wegener’s theory of continental drift and modern-day plate tectonics are enormous.

questions.title: Questions 31–36
instructions: Complete the summary using the list of phrases, A–J, below.
Write the correct letter, A–J, in boxes 31–36 on your answer sheet.

items.prompt: One of the remarkable things about Wegener from a 31 ____ is that although he proposed a theory of continental drift, he was not a geologist.
His 32 ____ were limited to atmospheric physics.
However, at the time he proposed his theory of continental drift in 1912, he was already a person of 33 ____ .
Six years previously, there had been his 34 ____ of 52 hours in a hot-air balloon,
followed by his well-publicised but 35 ____ of Greenland’s coast.
With the publication of his textbook on thermodynamics, he had also come to the attention of a 36 ____ of German scientists.

questions.title: Questions 37–40
instructions: Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
Write the correct letter in boxes 37–40 on your answer sheet.

items.prompt:
What is Mott T Greene doing in the fifth paragraph?
A describing what motivated him to write the book
B explaining why it is desirable to read the whole book
C suggesting why Wegener pursued so many different careers
D indicating what aspects of Wegener’s life interested him most

What is said about Wegener in the sixth paragraph?
A He was not a particularly ambitious person.
B He kept a record of all his scientific observations.
C He did not adopt many of the scientific practices of the time.
D He enjoyed discussing new discoveries with other scientists.

What does Greene say about some other famous scientists?
A Their published works had a greater impact than Wegener’s did.
B They had fewer doubts about their scientific ideas than Wegener did.
C Their scientific ideas were more controversial than Wegener’s.
D They are easier subjects to write about than Wegener.

What is Greene’s main point in the final paragraph?
A It is not enough in life to have good intentions.
B People need to plan carefully if they want to succeed.
C People have little control over many aspects of their lives.
D It is important that people ensure they have the freedom to act.
